outcomes: Applicable internal and external parties are informed about the ongoing containment measures

subtasks.title: Notify regulatory agencies
subtasks.content: Notified the appropriate regulatory agencies and affected individuals in accordance with the applicable laws and regulations identified during the Impact Assessment of the Identification stage.

subtasks.title: Use ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Referred to the ORNA Communications Templates section for guidance on the wording.

subtasks.title: Advise on privilege
subtasks.content: Continued advising the CIRT members on proper communications methods to ensure maintaining continuous client-attorney privilege where possible.

tasks.title: Protect Backups
tasks.content: The most critical aspect of ransomware recovery is having trusted backups, which will need immediate attention to ensure that they are protected and not overwritten.

subtasks.title: Analyze backup processes
subtasks.content: Analyzed backup processes for specific systems and data that could have been impacted.

subtasks.title: Check backup snapshots
subtasks.content: Checked status of backup snapshots.

tasks.title: Protect Active Directory
tasks.content: Many organizations utilize Active Directory (AD) to centrally manage user credentials and network resources. Ransomware attacks often exploit AD to obtain privileged credentials.
outcomes: AD compromise and lateral movement is contained
All AD accounts have new credentials enabled

subtasks.title: Restore or rebuild AD
subtasks.content: Restored AD from backup or rebuilt from scratch if no backup was available which predates the initial compromise.

subtasks.title: Reset KRBTGT account
subtasks.content: Completed the reset of the credentials for the Microsoft Kerberos account.

subtasks.title: Reset all AD credentials
subtasks.content: Completed the reset of all other AD account credentials.

phases.name: Eradication
phases.description: The Eradication stage focuses on removing all traces of the threat (such as malware) from the affected assets, and ensuring that the root cause has been eliminated.

tasks.title: Consider Establishing Contact With the Adversary
tasks.content: Whether or not to establish contact and/or attempt negotiating with the adversary is a complex decision based on multiple factors. The FBI advises victims to avoid negotiating with hackers.
outcomes: A decision is made as to whether to contact the threat actor

subtasks.title: Retain professional negotiator
subtasks.content: Retained the services of a professional negotiator, or delegate this task to your cyber insurance provider.

subtasks.title: Decide on contact
subtasks.content: If retaining professional negotiation services or delegating the task to the cyber insurance provider is not possible, consulted the Legal Counsel, CFO, CIRT Leader, law enforcement (if applicable), and other business stakeholders, and decided whether contact with the threat actor should be established.

subtasks.title: Follow ransom note instructions
subtasks.content: If the decision was made to start the negotiations with the threat actor, followed the instructions provided in the ransom note to establish contact.

subtasks.title: Follow negotiation practices
subtasks.content: Followed the recommended negotiation practices to communicate with the attacker and attempt to reduce the ransom amount as much as possible.

tasks.title: Attempt to Obtain an Existing Decryption Key
tasks.content: Depending on the ransomware variant, a known decryption key developed by security researchers and organizations may be freely available on the Internet.
outcomes: An existing decryption key may be obtained

subtasks.title: Search for decryption key
subtasks.content: Searched the Internet for an existing decryption key for the ransomware variant involved in this incident. Consulted: Avast, Emsisoft, NoMoreRansom, Kaspersky decryption tools.

tasks.title: Identify and Mitigate Vulnerabilities
tasks.content: Once the affected systems have been fully rebuilt, it is important to identify any vulnerabilities that may be present on the systems, preventing similar attacks from happening in the future.

subtasks.title: Run vulnerability scan
subtasks.content: Ran a vulnerability scan on the entire network and the surrounding infrastructure using specialized vulnerability scanning tools, such as: Nessus by Tenable, Qualys, OpenVAS.

subtasks.title: Create mitigation plan
subtasks.content: Created a prioritized plan to implement all relevant mitigation recommendations provided by the vulnerability scanning tools. Prioritized patches which have known exploits.

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved forensic evidence of any discovered vulnerabilities using the Artifact Upload feature of this Task.

tasks.title: Block and Remove Ransomware Email
tasks.content: If the ransomware was introduced into the infrastructure via a phishing email, it is important to block the email and remove it from all users' mailboxes.
outcomes: Phishing ransomware email is blocked and removed
Employee solicitation emails are blocked and removed
Emails utilizing domains with phishing or solicitation emails are blocked

subtasks.title: Delete phishing emails
subtasks.content: If the ransomware was received via a phishing email, deleted all similar phishing emails from all users' mailboxes. Additionally, configured your email gateway to block the types of attachments, keywords, senders or domains related to the malware.

subtasks.title: Create spam filters
subtasks.content: Created spam filters within email security settings to block the ransomware email and ensured that no further infections can occur.

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved all forensic evidence by using the Upload Artifact feature of this Task.

subtasks.title: Delete solicitation emails
subtasks.content: If employees were solicited via email to perform an insider ransomware attack, deleted all similar solicitation emails from all users' mailboxes.

tasks.title: Block Malicious Website
tasks.content: If the ransomware was introduced to the infrastructure via a malicious website or an attachment download, it is important to block the associated website at the network perimeter.
outcomes: Malicious websites are blocked at the network perimeter
DNS Sinkholes and Filters are used to block access to malicious websites

subtasks.title: Block malicious website
subtasks.content: If the ransomware was received via a malicious website, blocked the website at the network perimeter.

subtasks.title: Use DNS Sinkhole
subtasks.content: Used DNS Sinkhole and Filtering techniques to block access to malicious websites by configuring the DNS forwarder and blocking access to sites with malicious domain names/URLs.

subtasks.title: Block IP address
subtasks.content: Blocked the site's IP address via the network firewall.

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved all forensic evidence by using the Upload Artifact feature of this Task.

tasks.title: Continue Monitoring
tasks.content: There may still be unidentified Indicators of Compromise (IoCs). The network and systems should continue to be monitored for lateral movement, privilege escalation attempts and unauthorized access attempts.

subtasks.title: Investigate logging alerts
subtasks.content: Investigated logging alerts from network infrastructure, system and security tool logs to detect any added compromise or lateral movement.

phases.name: Recovery
phases.description: The Recovery stage aims to restore the affected assets to their full business-as-usual operation in the shortest possible time frame, after eradicating all traces of the threat.

tasks.title: Recover Encrypted Data Using Decryption Key
tasks.content: Perform the decryption process using local access to the machine, and NOT over the network. Even a momentary network outage can cause the decryption process to break and corrupt the files permanently.

subtasks.title: Follow decryption instructions
subtasks.content: Followed the instructions provided alongside the decryption key, either by the threat actor, or by the validated security research entity to recover the encrypted files. Repeated this step for each of the affected assets.

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved all forensic evidence by using the Upload Artifact feature of this Task.

subtasks.title: Contact threat actors if needed
subtasks.content: If decryption key did not recover data, contacted the threat actors for support on how to decrypt the data.

tasks.title: Restore and Recreate the Data
tasks.content: When the organization chooses not to pay the ransom it will need to recover the impacted data using existing, clean backups or recreate the data.
outcomes: All encrypted and inaccessible data have been restored from backups
All encrypted and inaccessible data have been rebuilt and recreated

subtasks.title: Restore from backups
subtasks.content: Restored the data from known clean backups (e.g. a cold storage backup).

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved all forensic evidence by using the Upload Artifact feature of this Task.

subtasks.title: Recreate data
subtasks.content: Recreated the data that has been encrypted and is no longer available.

tasks.title: Confirm Systems are Functioning Normally
tasks.content: Once the data is restored (whether using a backup, or via using a decryption key), it is important to verify that all restored systems are functioning as expected, and no additional malware is present.
outcomes: All systems are functioning as expected

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved all forensic evidence by using the Upload Artifact feature of this Task.

subtasks.title: Re-run malware scans
subtasks.content: Re-ran malware scans on all systems to confirm that the infection did not persist.

subtasks.title: Run vulnerability scans
subtasks.content: Ran vulnerability scans on the freshly restored systems to confirm that there are no malicious binaries or security vulnerabilities.

subtasks.title: Mitigate vulnerabilities
subtasks.content: Mitigated all relevant discovered vulnerabilities (if any).

subtasks.title: Reconnect to network
subtasks.content: Connected the restored systems back to the network.

tasks.title: Remediate the Root Cause(s)
tasks.content: Using all the information collected during the Identification and Containment stages, perform a root cause analysis to determine the initial entry point of the ransomware and remediate the vulnerability.
outcomes: Root cause analysis is performed and initial entry points are established

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved all forensic evidence by using the Upload Artifact feature of this Task.

subtasks.title: Apply security patches
subtasks.content: If a vulnerability was exploited to provide the attacker the initial access to the systems, applied all relevant security patches to the affected systems or software.

subtasks.title: Implement robust configurations
subtasks.content: If a weak security configuration was exploited (e.g. an Internet-facing RDP port, weak account credentials, misconfigured firewall rules, etc.), implemented more robust configuration settings to the device, account, or software.

tasks.title: Normalize Affected Networks
tasks.content: During the Containment and Eradication stages, certain network communication and/or file-sharing protocols may have been disabled. Make sure to restore all previously disabled network protocols and/or suspended user accounts.
outcomes: All networks, protocols and user accounts are restored

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved all forensic evidence by using the Upload Artifact feature of this Task.

subtasks.title: Restore services
subtasks.content: Restored any services that were suspended during the Containment and Eradication stages.

subtasks.title: Restore user accounts
subtasks.content: Restored any user accounts that were suspended during the Containment and Eradication stages.

subtasks.title: Issue password resets
subtasks.content: Issued password resets for all affected systems and addressed any associated vulnerabilities and gaps in security or visibility with system alerts and notifications.

tasks.title: Issue External and Internal Communications Updates
tasks.content: Once the affected systems are restored and the organization returns to Business As Usual (BAU), it is important to issue external and internal communications updates.
outcomes: External and internal communication updates are issued

subtasks.title: Issue internal communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to internal stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

subtasks.title: Issue external communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to external stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

tasks.title: Issue Mandatory Regulatory Notifications or Updates
tasks.content: Issuing regular updates about the progress of the incident resolution activities may be required based on the incident severity, affected data, and the applicable laws and regulations.
outcomes: Applicable parties are informed about the ongoing recovery measures

subtasks.title: Notify regulatory agencies
subtasks.content: Notified the appropriate regulatory agencies and the affected individuals in accordance with the applicable laws and regulations as was done during the Containment stage.

subtasks.title: Advise on privilege
subtasks.content: Continued advising the CIRT members on proper communications methods within and outside the War Room to ensure maintaining continuous client-attorney privilege where possible.

subtasks.title: Use ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Referred to the ORNA Communications Templates section for guidance on the wording.

tasks.title: Rebuild Affected Systems
tasks.content: If the organization has decided to proceed with the systems rebuild using the existing backup solution, begin the process of a full rebuild of the affected systems.

subtasks.title: Rebuild from clean media
subtasks.content: Rebuilt affected systems from known clean media

subtasks.title: Confirm antivirus up to date
subtasks.content: Confirmed that antivirus is up to date and enabled on all systems.

subtasks.title: Confirm patches deployed
subtasks.content: Confirmed that applicable patches are deployed on all systems.

tasks.title: Decide Whether to Pay the Ransom
tasks.content: Whether or not to pay the ransom is a complex decision influenced by a multitude of factors. It is absolutely imperative to consult with the Legal Counsel, your insurance provider and in some cases law enforcement agencies.
outcomes: A Decision Regarding the Ransom Payout is Made

subtasks.title: Evaluate restore capability
subtasks.content: Evaluated the organization's ability to restore lost/encrypted data without paying the ransom. Consulted with CFO, CIRT Leader, Core CIRT members, Managed Security Services, Digital Forensics Investigator and Third Party IT providers.

subtasks.title: Evaluate business impact
subtasks.content: Evaluated the business impact of restoring the data without paying the ransom. Reviewed the existing BC/DR plan and consulted with the CFO, CIRT Leader, Legal Counsel considering RPO, RTO, and data loss tolerance.

subtasks.title: Evaluate impact if not paid
subtasks.content: Evaluated the business, financial, regulatory and reputational impact to the organization if the ransom is not paid. Considered data exfiltration, PII/PHI compromise, trade secrets exposure, and customer trust.

subtasks.title: Evaluate risks of paying
subtasks.content: Evaluated the risks associated with the option of paying the ransom. Considered whether decryption key will be provided, if data will be destroyed, if organization can afford to pay, if it's legal to pay, and if it's ethically acceptable.

tasks.title: Pay the Ransom and Obtain the Decryption Key
tasks.content: If the decision was made to proceed with the ransom payout, follow the instructions provided by the attacker to make the payment.
outcomes: The decryption key is obtained from the threat actor
Obtained proof that any exfiltrated data has been destroyed

subtasks.title: Source ransom funds
subtasks.content: Coordinated with the CEO/COO and consulted with the Legal Counsel, insurance provider and law enforcement (if applicable) to source the funds required for the ransom payout.

subtasks.title: Create cryptocurrency wallet
subtasks.content: Worked with the CIRT Leader and Core CIRT Members to create and configure a cryptocurrency wallet for the organization based on the type of currency requested by the attacker.

subtasks.title: Transfer funds
subtasks.content: Loaded the funds to the cryptocurrency wallet and made the transfer following the instructions provided by the attacker.

subtasks.title: Obtain decryption key
subtasks.content: Obtained the decryption key to regain access to any encrypted data and, if applicable, any proof that sensitive exfiltrated data in the threat actors' possession has been destroyed.

tasks.title: Scan the Decryption Key
tasks.content: If the organization decided to pay the ransom amount and received the decryption key from the attacker, it is crucial to scan the decryption key for any hidden malware or backdoors.
outcomes: The existence or absence of backdoors or malware confirmed

subtasks.title: Reverse-engineer decryption key
subtasks.content: Consulted with the digital forensics investigator and reverse-engineered the decryption key provided by the attacker to look for built-in malware and/or backdoors.

subtasks.title: Employ 3rd party vendor
subtasks.content: Employed a 3rd party vendor that specializes in ransomware removal and decryption services to analyze the decryption key.

subtasks.title: Test in sandbox
subtasks.content: If the analysis of the decryption key is not feasible, test it out in an isolated (e.g. sandbox) environment and subsequently scan the environment for malware.

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved all relevant forensic evidence by using the Upload Artifact feature of this Task.

tasks.title: Resolve Incident
tasks.content: Once the affected systems and data are restored to normal business operations the incident can be moved to the 'resolved' state.
outcomes: The incident is declared as resolved

subtasks.title: Verify systems back to normal
subtasks.content: Verified with Core CIRT Members and IT staff that all systems and configurations are back to normal business functions.

subtasks.title: Change incident status
subtasks.content: Changed status of incident to 'resolved'.

phases.name: Lessons Learned
phases.description: The Lessons Learned stage's main goal is to compile all relevant information about the incident and extract lessons that can help with future incident response activity.

tasks.title: Generate Incident Report
tasks.content: Preparing an incident report is an important (and often mandatory) part of the cyber incident response process. This report will be used to perform business impact analysis, comply with the applicable laws and regulations, and update relevant stakeholders.

subtasks.title: Generate report
subtasks.content: Once the Incident is marked as Resolved, use the Reports tab to generate and view the report.

subtasks.title: Review for accuracy
subtasks.content: Reviewed the report for accuracy and address any errors/issues with the team member(s) responsible for the task in question.

subtasks.title: Ensure artifacts included
subtasks.content: Ensured all relevant artifacts are included for each task.

subtasks.title: Share report
subtasks.content: Downloaded or export the report and share it with the appropriate stakeholders alongside its unique secure access password (available in the Reports tab).

subtasks.title: Retain report
subtasks.content: Ensured that the Incident report and all related evidence are retained in accordance with legal and regulatory requirements.

tasks.title: Fulfill External and Internal Reporting Obligations
tasks.content: Once the incident is resolved and the investigation is complete, an organization may be required to deliver the incident report (or its appropriate sections) to the regulatory agencies, affected companies or individuals, law enforcement or other stakeholders.
outcomes: The Incident Report (or its appropriate sections) is shared with the applicable parties
Applicable regulatory and legal obligations are fulfilled

subtasks.title: Obtain and review report
subtasks.content: Obtained the full Incident Report from the CIRT Leader and review it for accuracy.

subtasks.title: Identify obligations
subtasks.content: Identified all applicable legal and regulatory requirements related to cyber incident reporting.

subtasks.title: Deliver report
subtasks.content: Delivered the Incident report to the appropriate legal and/or regulatory agencies and the affected individuals, in accordance with the applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

tasks.title: Host the Lessons Learned Session
tasks.content: The goal of the Lessons Learned session is to learn from the incident, identify any policy, process or procedure gaps that were revealed during the handling of the incident, and to create initiatives to improve the organizations' security posture.
outcomes: The Lessons Learned session is conducted
Gaps and areas for improvement are identified
Initiatives are created to operationalize identified improvements

subtasks.title: Prepare presentation
subtasks.content: Prepared a presentation for the Lessons Learned meeting, containing: when incident was detected and by whom, scope of incident, response steps taken, areas where CIRT was effective, gaps and improvement opportunities, prevention steps.

subtasks.title: Host session
subtasks.content: Hosted the Lessons Learned session. Make sure that all CIRT members attend and actively participate in the session.

subtasks.title: Establish open dialogue
subtasks.content: Established an open dialogue which discussed: how well staff handled incident, if procedures were followed, if procedures were adequate, what held back recovery, what needs to be done differently, if 3rd parties were responsive, what policy modifications are needed, what training is required, what tools and resources are needed.

subtasks.title: Document improvements
subtasks.content: Documented all identified areas for improvement and prepared a list of agreed upon action items.

subtasks.title: Distribute action items
subtasks.content: Distributed the action items list among relevant CIRT members and ensured that the improvement steps are implemented.

tasks.title: Perform Employee Security Awareness Training
tasks.content: Whether the incident resulted from a phishing campaign, configuration error or from an exploited vulnerability, it is always a good idea to conduct an employee awareness training session after any incident is resolved.
outcomes: The employee awareness training is conducted
A roadmap for table top exercises is developed based on lessons learnt

subtasks.title: Prepare educational materials
subtasks.content: Prepared educational materials for the employee awareness training, with emphasis on the recent incident and the steps employees can take to help the organization avoid similar incidents in the future.

subtasks.title: Track attendance
subtasks.content: Tracked employee attendance using a Learning Management System (LMS).

subtasks.title: Work with 3rd party
subtasks.content: Where applicable, worked with a 3rd party to prepare and conduct the employee security awareness training.

subtasks.title: Develop table top exercises
subtasks.content: Used the outcomes from the Lessons Learned part of the Incident Management Process to develop a table top exercise plan with relevant scenarios and participants to practice any new processes developed post incident.

name: Network IoT Incident Response
description: Comprehensive playbook for responding to network and IoT security incidents including unauthorized access, compromised devices, and network-based attacks.

phases.name: Identification
phases.description: The purpose of the Identification stage is to accurately determine the Incident type, severity and perform immediate containment and escalation.

tasks.title: Initiate CIRT Team Meeting and Execute Escalation Procedures
tasks.content: As more information about the incident is known, the core CIRT team will meet and define which organizational SMEs need to be included to respond to the incident.
outcomes: First Core CIRT meeting is held with invited SMEs
OOB communications channels are set up and periodic checkins scheduled
Privileged Communications is established with legal counsel

subtasks.title: Initiate first CIRT meeting
subtasks.content: Initiated first meeting of the organizational core CIRT.

subtasks.title: Notify CIRT team members
subtasks.content: Notified CIRT team members using the contacts and role assignments on the My Team page and made sure ORNA roles are assigned so they are aware of the tasks allocated for them.

subtasks.title: Verify Incident Severity
subtasks.content: Verified the Incident Severity determined during the Incident record creation process as promptly and accurately as possible. The severity may change as more details are uncovered and it will need to be updated when the situation changes.

subtasks.title: Invite additional SMEs
subtasks.content: Invited additional SMEs to be included in CIRT update meetings.

subtasks.title: Set up secured meeting room
subtasks.content: Set up a secured meeting room (either physical and/or virtual).

subtasks.title: Schedule follow-up meetings
subtasks.content: Scheduled follow-up meetings according to a defined cadence.

subtasks.title: Work with Legal Counsel
subtasks.content: Worked closely with Legal Counsel to retain client-attorney privilege over the information pertaining to the incident and ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

subtasks.title: Create initial incident assessment
subtasks.content: Created an initial incident assessment to share with organizational leaders. This assessment notification will contain a summary of what has occurred; date & time of incident; scope of compromise; data involved; actions taken so far and next steps recipients can expect to see.

subtasks.title: Establish log of meetings
subtasks.content: Established a log of items covered to keep track of when meetings occurred, who was present, high level discussion notes and follow-up actions.

tasks.title: Validate Network or IoT Incident
tasks.content: Network incidents refer to security events that occur at the network layer including unauthorized network access, network scanning, sniffing or eavesdropping. IoT incidents involve compromised IoT devices such as smart cameras, sensors, or other connected devices.
outcomes: A security incident is confirmed as a network or IoT incident

subtasks.title: Review network logs
subtasks.content: Reviewed network logs, firewall logs, IDS/IPS alerts, and router logs for suspicious activity.

subtasks.title: Analyze IoT device behavior
subtasks.content: Analyzed IoT device behavior for anomalies including unexpected network connections, unusual data transfers, or changes in device configuration.

subtasks.title: Check for unauthorized access
subtasks.content: Checked for signs of unauthorized network access including unknown MAC addresses, rogue access points, or unauthorized VPN connections.

subtasks.title: Verify network scanning activity
subtasks.content: Verified whether network scanning or reconnaissance activity has been detected.

tasks.title: Determine Scope of Incident
tasks.content: Identify all affected network segments, devices, and systems. It is important to collect as much relevant information as possible about all assets that are affected.
outcomes: All affected network segments are identified
All affected devices and systems are identified

subtasks.title: Identify affected network segments
subtasks.content: Identified which network segments were affected by reviewing network topology maps, VLAN configurations, and firewall rules.

subtasks.title: Catalog affected devices
subtasks.content: Cataloged all affected devices including servers, workstations, IoT devices, network equipment, and their criticality to business operations.

subtasks.title: Analyze network traffic
subtasks.content: Analyzed network traffic patterns using packet capture tools and network monitoring systems to identify anomalous communications.

subtasks.title: Review device inventory
subtasks.content: Reviewed device inventory and compared against known authorized devices to identify rogue or unauthorized devices on the network.

tasks.title: Assess Need for External Support
tasks.content: After an initial assessment of the scope of the network or IoT incident, the organization will have to make a determination as to whether outside help is needed and for what functions.
outcomes: Decision is made whether to hire external technical help
Decision is made whether to hire external legal help
ORNA incident response expertise is utilized if needed

subtasks.title: Consider network forensics expert
subtasks.content: Considered retaining a network forensics expert to help analyze network traffic and identify attack vectors.

subtasks.title: Consider IoT security specialist
subtasks.content: Considered retaining an IoT security specialist if IoT devices are involved in the incident.

subtasks.title: Consider outside legal counsel
subtasks.content: Considered retaining outside legal counsel if sensitive data or regulatory compliance is involved.

subtasks.title: Consider ORNA support
subtasks.content: Considered retaining ORNA for incident response expertise.

tasks.title: Assess Impact
tasks.content: Assess the impact to prioritize actions. The impacts should be assessed for operational business impact, regulatory impact, and reputational impact.

subtasks.title: Assess business impact
subtasks.content: Assessed impact to business or mission: How critical are the affected network segments and devices? How much disruption has occurred? What services are degraded or unavailable?

subtasks.title: Assess data compromise
subtasks.content: Assessed whether any sensitive data was accessed, exfiltrated, or compromised through network or IoT devices.

subtasks.title: Assess regulatory impact
subtasks.content: Assessed regulatory impact: What is the regulatory status of any compromised data (e.g., PII, ePHI)?

subtasks.title: Assess reputational damage
subtasks.content: Assessed potential reputational damage from the network or IoT security incident.

tasks.title: Determine Root Cause(s)
tasks.content: Identify how the network or IoT incident occurred. Understanding the root cause is critical for effective remediation.

subtasks.title: Analyze attack vectors
subtasks.content: Analyzed potential attack vectors including: weak or default credentials on IoT devices, unpatched vulnerabilities in network devices, misconfigured firewalls or access controls, rogue access points, or compromised VPN credentials.

subtasks.title: Review authentication logs
subtasks.content: Reviewed authentication logs to determine if unauthorized access occurred and how credentials were obtained.

subtasks.title: Check for exploited vulnerabilities
subtasks.content: Checked whether known vulnerabilities in network devices or IoT firmware were exploited.

subtasks.title: Identify configuration weaknesses
subtasks.content: Identified configuration weaknesses including default passwords, unnecessary open ports, or missing network segmentation.

tasks.title: Send Communications
tasks.content: Communications will need to exist for both internal and external parties. Timely and controlled external communications play a crucial role in managing the narrative.
outcomes: Approved external parties are contacted and notified of incident
Decision made whether to utilize ORNA templates for communication
Employees are notified about the incident and instructed on the next steps

subtasks.title: Work with Legal Counsel
subtasks.content: Worked with Legal Counsel and provided all available details about the incident severity, impact and the type of information involved, if known.

subtasks.title: Craft external messages
subtasks.content: Worked closely with Legal Counsel and Communications Team to craft messages to external parties to be notified.

subtasks.title: Craft internal messages
subtasks.content: Worked closely with Legal Counsel and Communications Team to craft messages for internal staff.

subtasks.title: Utilize ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Utilized ORNA communications templates where appropriate.

subtasks.title: Publish external communications
subtasks.content: Published external communications in line with the communications strategy.

subtasks.title: Work on communications cadence
subtasks.content: Worked with communications team and legal counsel on a cadence for updating external and internal communications.

tasks.title: Invoke Data Breach Notification Protocols
tasks.content: When a cyber incident is suspected or confirmed that may involve data compromise, it is important that the Legal Counsel proceeds swiftly to understand the type of data affected.
outcomes: The type of breach and affected data are established and confirmed
Appropriate data breach notification protocols are invoked

subtasks.title: Confirm data impact
subtasks.content: Confirmed whether Personal Identifiable Information (PII), Protected Health Information (PHI), or financial information is impacted.

subtasks.title: Examine regulatory obligations
subtasks.content: Examined regulatory obligations.

subtasks.title: Notify appropriate parties
subtasks.content: Notified the appropriate regulatory agencies and affected individuals as prescribed by relevant laws, regulations and contractual obligations.

subtasks.title: Advise on client-attorney privilege
subtasks.content: Advised the CIRT members on proper communication methods to ensure continuous client-attorney privilege where possible.

phases.name: Containment
phases.description: The main objective of the Containment stage is to limit the impact of the incident promptly and as much as possible, preventing further damage to the organization's assets.

tasks.title: Isolate Affected Network Segments and Devices
tasks.content: Immediately isolate affected network segments and devices to prevent lateral movement and further compromise.

subtasks.title: Isolate network segments
subtasks.content: Isolated affected network segments using VLANs, firewall rules, or physical disconnection to prevent lateral movement.

subtasks.title: Disconnect compromised devices
subtasks.content: Disconnected compromised IoT devices and systems from the network while preserving forensic evidence.

subtasks.title: Block malicious traffic
subtasks.content: Configured firewalls and IPS to block known malicious IP addresses and traffic patterns.

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved forensic evidence before making changes to network configurations.

tasks.title: Disable Compromised Accounts
tasks.content: Disable any user or service accounts that have been compromised or are suspected of being compromised.

subtasks.title: Identify compromised accounts
subtasks.content: Identified compromised user and service accounts through log analysis.

subtasks.title: Disable accounts
subtasks.content: Disabled compromised accounts in Active Directory, LDAP, or other authentication systems.

subtasks.title: Revoke access tokens
subtasks.content: Revoked any active sessions, tokens, or certificates associated with compromised accounts.

tasks.title: Block Malicious Network Traffic
tasks.content: Block malicious network traffic at the perimeter and internal network boundaries.
outcomes: Command and control communications are disrupted

subtasks.title: Update firewall rules
subtasks.content: Updated firewall rules to block known malicious IP addresses, domains, and ports.

subtasks.title: Configure IPS signatures
subtasks.content: Configured IPS/IDS signatures to detect and block attack traffic.

subtasks.title: Block C2 communications
subtasks.content: Blocked command and control (C2) server communications if malware or botnet activity is detected.

subtasks.title: Update DNS filtering
subtasks.content: Updated DNS filtering to block known malicious domains.

tasks.title: Invoke Business Continuity Plan
tasks.content: To avoid major disruptions of key business functions, invoke the Business Continuity Plan (BCP), if such exists, in accordance with the incident severity and impact.
outcomes: If it exists the Business Continuity Plan is invoked
If no Business Continuity Plan exists a war room is used to create one
Situational business continuity procedures are established

subtasks.title: Invoke BCP communication
subtasks.content: Issued the communication and informed the appropriate stakeholders that the BCP should be invoked to minimize business disruption.

subtasks.title: Proceed with BCP procedures
subtasks.content: Proceeded in accordance with the BCP procedures.

subtasks.title: Establish war room if needed
subtasks.content: Established a war room if no BCP existed for affected systems and networks.

tasks.title: Issue External and Internal Communications Update
tasks.content: Issuing periodic updates on the progress of the incident response activities will help control the narrative.
outcomes: Internal stakeholders are informed about the ongoing containment measures
External stakeholders are informed about the ongoing containment measures

subtasks.title: Issue internal communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to internal stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

subtasks.title: Issue external communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to external stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

subtasks.title: Use ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Referred to the ORNA Communications Templates section for guidance on the wording.

tasks.title: Issue Mandatory Regulatory Notifications or Updates
tasks.content: Issuing periodic updates about the progress of the incident response activities may be required based on the incident severity and the applicable laws, regulations and contractual obligations.
outcomes: Applicable internal and external parties are informed about the ongoing containment measures

subtasks.title: Notify regulatory agencies
subtasks.content: Notified the appropriate regulatory agencies and affected individuals in accordance with the applicable laws and regulations.

subtasks.title: Use ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Referred to the ORNA Communications Templates section for guidance on the wording.

subtasks.title: Advise on privilege
subtasks.content: Continued advising the CIRT members on proper communications methods to ensure maintaining continuous client-attorney privilege where possible.

phases.name: Eradication
phases.description: The Eradication stage focuses on removing all traces of the threat from the affected assets, and ensuring that the root cause has been eliminated.

tasks.title: Remove Unauthorized Devices and Access Points
tasks.content: Remove any unauthorized or rogue devices, access points, or network equipment from the network.
outcomes: All unauthorized devices are removed from the network

subtasks.title: Scan for unauthorized devices
subtasks.content: Scanned the network for unauthorized devices using network discovery tools and MAC address filtering.

subtasks.title: Remove rogue devices
subtasks.content: Physically removed or disabled rogue access points, unauthorized switches, or other network equipment.

subtasks.title: Update device inventory
subtasks.content: Updated the authorized device inventory and implemented controls to prevent unauthorized device connections.

tasks.title: Patch and Update Vulnerable Systems
tasks.content: Apply security patches and updates to all vulnerable network devices, IoT devices, and systems.

subtasks.title: Identify vulnerable systems
subtasks.content: Identified all vulnerable network devices, routers, switches, firewalls, and IoT devices requiring patches.

subtasks.title: Apply security patches
subtasks.content: Applied security patches to network infrastructure and IoT devices according to vendor recommendations.

subtasks.title: Update IoT firmware
subtasks.content: Updated firmware on all IoT devices to the latest secure versions.

subtasks.title: Verify patch deployment
subtasks.content: Verified that all patches were successfully deployed and systems are functioning properly.

tasks.title: Reset Compromised Credentials
tasks.content: Reset all credentials that were compromised or potentially exposed during the incident.

subtasks.title: Reset user passwords
subtasks.content: Reset passwords for all compromised user accounts.

subtasks.title: Reset IoT device credentials
subtasks.content: Changed default credentials on all IoT devices to strong, unique passwords.

subtasks.title: Reset service account credentials
subtasks.content: Reset service account credentials and API keys that may have been exposed.

subtasks.title: Implement MFA
subtasks.content: Implemented multi-factor authentication (MFA) for network access where not already in place.

tasks.title: Harden Network and IoT Configurations
tasks.content: Implement security hardening measures on network devices and IoT systems to prevent future incidents.

subtasks.title: Implement network segmentation
subtasks.content: Implemented or improved network segmentation to isolate IoT devices and critical systems.

subtasks.title: Disable unnecessary services
subtasks.content: Disabled unnecessary services, ports, and protocols on network devices and IoT systems.

subtasks.title: Configure access controls
subtasks.content: Configured strict access controls and implemented the principle of least privilege.

subtasks.title: Enable security logging
subtasks.content: Enabled comprehensive security logging on all network devices and IoT systems.

tasks.title: Remove Malware and Backdoors
tasks.content: If malware or backdoors were installed during the incident, ensure they are completely removed from all affected systems.

subtasks.title: Scan for malware
subtasks.content: Performed comprehensive malware scans on all affected network devices and systems.

subtasks.title: Remove identified threats
subtasks.content: Removed or quarantined all identified malware, rootkits, and backdoors.

subtasks.title: Factory reset IoT devices
subtasks.content: Performed factory reset on compromised IoT devices if malware cannot be reliably removed.

subtasks.title: Verify clean state
subtasks.content: Verified that systems are clean through multiple scanning methods and behavioral analysis.

phases.name: Recovery
phases.description: The Recovery stage aims to restore the affected assets to their full business-as-usual operation in the shortest possible time frame, after eradicating all traces of the threat.

tasks.title: Restore Network Services
tasks.content: Gradually restore network services and connectivity while monitoring for any signs of recurring issues.

subtasks.title: Test network connectivity
subtasks.content: Tested network connectivity and services in isolated segments before full restoration.

subtasks.title: Gradually restore services
subtasks.content: Gradually restored network services starting with the most critical business functions.

subtasks.title: Monitor for issues
subtasks.content: Continuously monitored restored services for any signs of recurring issues or anomalies.

subtasks.title: Verify functionality
subtasks.content: Verified that all network services are functioning as expected and performance is normal.

tasks.title: Reconnect IoT Devices
tasks.content: Safely reconnect IoT devices to the network after ensuring they are properly secured and configured.

subtasks.title: Verify IoT device security
subtasks.content: Verified that all IoT devices are properly patched, configured, and have strong credentials before reconnection.

subtasks.title: Reconnect to isolated network
subtasks.content: Reconnected IoT devices to a properly segmented and monitored network segment.

subtasks.title: Test device functionality
subtasks.content: Tested each IoT device to ensure it functions properly and does not exhibit suspicious behavior.

subtasks.title: Monitor device activity
subtasks.content: Implemented continuous monitoring of IoT device network activity and behavior.

tasks.title: Re-enable User Access
tasks.content: Re-enable user access to network resources after verifying that the environment is secure.

subtasks.title: Re-enable user accounts
subtasks.content: Re-enabled previously disabled user accounts after password resets and security verification.

subtasks.title: Verify MFA enrollment
subtasks.content: Verified that all users have enrolled in multi-factor authentication if newly implemented.

subtasks.title: Grant phased access
subtasks.content: Granted access in phases, starting with critical users and monitoring for any issues.

subtasks.title: Communicate access procedures
subtasks.content: Communicated new access procedures and security requirements to all users.

tasks.title: Validate Security Controls
tasks.content: Validate that all security controls are functioning properly and providing adequate protection.

subtasks.title: Test firewall rules
subtasks.content: Tested firewall rules to ensure they are properly blocking unauthorized traffic.

subtasks.title: Verify IDS/IPS functionality
subtasks.content: Verified that IDS/IPS systems are detecting and blocking attack patterns.

subtasks.title: Test access controls
subtasks.content: Tested network access controls and segmentation to ensure proper isolation.

subtasks.title: Validate logging and monitoring
subtasks.content: Validated that logging and monitoring systems are capturing all necessary security events.

tasks.title: Issue External and Internal Communications Updates
tasks.content: Once the affected systems are restored and the organization returns to Business As Usual (BAU), issue communications updates.
outcomes: External and internal communication updates are issued

subtasks.title: Issue internal communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to internal stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

subtasks.title: Issue external communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to external stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

tasks.title: Issue Mandatory Regulatory Notifications or Updates
tasks.content: Issuing regular updates about the progress of the incident resolution activities may be required based on the incident severity and the applicable laws and regulations.
outcomes: Applicable parties are informed about the ongoing recovery measures

subtasks.title: Notify regulatory agencies
subtasks.content: Notified the appropriate regulatory agencies and affected individuals in accordance with the applicable laws and regulations.

subtasks.title: Advise on privilege
subtasks.content: Continued advising the CIRT members on proper communications methods to ensure maintaining continuous client-attorney privilege where possible.

subtasks.title: Use ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Referred to the ORNA Communications Templates section for guidance on the wording.

tasks.title: Resolve Incident
tasks.content: Once the affected systems and network are restored to normal business operations, the incident can be moved to the 'resolved' state.
outcomes: The incident is declared as resolved

subtasks.title: Verify systems back to normal
subtasks.content: Verified with Core CIRT Members and IT staff that all systems and network services are back to normal business functions.

subtasks.title: Change incident status
subtasks.content: Changed status of incident to 'resolved'.

phases.name: Lessons Learned
phases.description: The Lessons Learned stage's main goal is to compile all relevant information about the incident and extract lessons that can help with future incident response activity.

tasks.title: Generate Incident Report
tasks.content: Preparing an incident report is an important part of the cyber incident response process. This report will be used to perform business impact analysis and comply with applicable laws and regulations.

subtasks.title: Generate report
subtasks.content: Once the Incident is marked as Resolved, used the Reports tab to generate and view the report.

subtasks.title: Review for accuracy
subtasks.content: Reviewed the report for accuracy and addressed any errors/issues with the team member(s) responsible for the task in question.

subtasks.title: Ensure artifacts included
subtasks.content: Ensured all relevant artifacts are included for each task.

subtasks.title: Share report
subtasks.content: Downloaded or exported the report and shared it with the appropriate stakeholders.

subtasks.title: Retain report
subtasks.content: Ensured that the Incident report and all related evidence are retained in accordance with legal and regulatory requirements.

tasks.title: Fulfill External and Internal Reporting Obligations
tasks.content: Once the incident is resolved and the investigation is complete, deliver the incident report to required parties.
outcomes: The Incident Report is shared with applicable parties
Regulatory and legal obligations are fulfilled

subtasks.title: Obtain and review report
subtasks.content: Obtained the full Incident Report from the CIRT Leader and reviewed it for accuracy.

subtasks.title: Identify obligations
subtasks.content: Identified all applicable legal and regulatory requirements related to cyber incident reporting.

subtasks.title: Deliver report
subtasks.content: Delivered the Incident report to the appropriate legal and/or regulatory agencies and affected individuals.

tasks.title: Host the Lessons Learned Session
tasks.content: The goal of the Lessons Learned session is to learn from the incident and identify policy, process or procedure gaps.
outcomes: The Lessons Learned session is conducted
Gaps and areas for improvement are identified
Initiatives are created to operationalize identified improvements

subtasks.title: Prepare presentation
subtasks.content: Prepared a presentation covering: when incident was detected, scope, response steps taken, areas where CIRT was effective, gaps and improvement opportunities, prevention steps.

subtasks.title: Host session
subtasks.content: Hosted the Lessons Learned session with all CIRT members actively participating.

subtasks.title: Establish open dialogue
subtasks.content: Established an open dialogue discussing: how well staff handled incident, if procedures were followed and adequate, what held back recovery, what needs to be done differently.

subtasks.title: Document improvements
subtasks.content: Documented all identified areas for improvement and prepared a list of agreed upon action items.

subtasks.title: Distribute action items
subtasks.content: Distributed the action items list among relevant CIRT members and ensured that the improvement steps are implemented.

tasks.title: Perform Employee Security Awareness Training
tasks.content: Conduct employee awareness training after the incident is resolved to prevent similar future incidents.
outcomes: A roadmap for table top exercises is developed

subtasks.title: Prepare educational materials
subtasks.content: Prepared educational materials emphasizing network and IoT security best practices.

subtasks.title: Track attendance
subtasks.content: Tracked employee attendance using a Learning Management System (LMS).

subtasks.title: Work with 3rd party
subtasks.content: Where applicable, worked with a 3rd party to prepare and conduct the employee security awareness training.

subtasks.title: Develop table top exercises
subtasks.content: Used the outcomes from the Lessons Learned to develop table top exercise plans with relevant scenarios.

name: Denial of Service Response
description: Comprehensive playbook for responding to Denial of Service (DoS) and Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks that disrupt or degrade network services and system availability.

phases.name: Identification
phases.description: The purpose of the Identification stage is to accurately determine the Incident type, severity and perform immediate containment and escalation.

tasks.title: Initiate CIRT Team Meeting and Execute Escalation Procedures
tasks.content: As more information about the incident is known, the core CIRT team will meet and define which organizational SMEs need to be included to respond to the incident.
outcomes: First Core CIRT meeting is held with invited SMEs
OOB communications channels are set up and periodic checkins scheduled
Privileged Communications is established with legal counsel

subtasks.title: Initiate first CIRT meeting
subtasks.content: Initiated first meeting of the organizational core CIRT.

subtasks.title: Notify CIRT team members
subtasks.content: Notified CIRT team members using the contacts and role assignments on the My Team page and made sure ORNA roles are assigned so they are aware of the tasks allocated for them.

subtasks.title: Verify Incident Severity
subtasks.content: Verified the Incident Severity determined during the Incident record creation process as promptly and accurately as possible. The severity may change as more details are uncovered and it will need to be updated when the situation changes.

subtasks.title: Invite additional SMEs
subtasks.content: Invited additional SMEs to be included in CIRT update meetings.

subtasks.title: Set up secured meeting room
subtasks.content: Set up a secured meeting room (either physical and/or virtual).

subtasks.title: Schedule follow-up meetings
subtasks.content: Scheduled follow-up meetings according to a defined cadence.

subtasks.title: Work with Legal Counsel
subtasks.content: Worked closely with Legal Counsel to retain client-attorney privilege over the information pertaining to the incident and ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

subtasks.title: Create initial incident assessment
subtasks.content: Created an initial incident assessment to share with organizational leaders. This assessment notification will contain a summary of what has occurred; date & time of incident; scope of compromise; data involved; actions taken so far and next steps recipients can expect to see.

subtasks.title: Establish log of meetings
subtasks.content: Established a log of items covered to keep track of when meetings occurred, who was present, high level discussion notes and follow-up actions.

tasks.title: Validate Denial of Service Incident
tasks.content: A Denial of Service (DoS) attack is an attempt to make a machine or network resource unavailable to its intended users. DoS attacks typically flood servers, systems or networks with traffic to overload resources. Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks use multiple compromised systems as sources of attack traffic.
outcomes: A security incident is confirmed as a DoS/DDoS attack
Attack type and vectors are identified

subtasks.title: Analyze network traffic patterns
subtasks.content: Analyzed network traffic patterns to identify abnormal spikes in volume, unusual protocols, or suspicious source addresses indicating a DoS/DDoS attack.

subtasks.title: Check service availability
subtasks.content: Checked availability and response times of critical services and systems to determine which resources are being targeted or affected.

subtasks.title: Review monitoring alerts
subtasks.content: Reviewed alerts from network monitoring tools, IDS/IPS, firewalls, and load balancers for indications of attack traffic.

subtasks.title: Identify attack type
subtasks.content: Identified the type of DoS/DDoS attack: volumetric (bandwidth exhaustion), protocol (SYN flood, fragmentation), or application-layer (HTTP flood, Slowloris).

tasks.title: Determine Attack Scope and Impact
tasks.content: Identify all affected services, systems, and network segments. Understanding the full scope is critical for effective response and mitigation.
outcomes: All affected services and systems are identified
Attack magnitude and characteristics are documented

subtasks.title: Identify targeted services
subtasks.content: Identified which services, applications, or network segments are being targeted by the attack.

subtasks.title: Measure attack volume
subtasks.content: Measured the volume and rate of attack traffic (packets per second, bits per second, requests per second).

subtasks.title: Analyze attack sources
subtasks.content: Analyzed source IP addresses, geographic locations, and patterns to understand if this is a simple DoS or distributed DDoS attack.

subtasks.title: Document attack vectors
subtasks.content: Documented the attack vectors being used (UDP floods, SYN floods, HTTP requests, DNS amplification, etc.).

tasks.title: Assess Need for External Support
tasks.content: Determine if external assistance is needed to effectively mitigate the attack, especially for large-scale DDoS attacks that may require ISP or cloud provider intervention.
outcomes: Decision is made whether to engage ISP or DDoS mitigation service
Decision is made whether to hire external technical help
ORNA incident response expertise is utilized if needed

subtasks.title: Contact ISP
subtasks.content: Contacted Internet Service Provider (ISP) for upstream filtering and traffic diversion if attack exceeds local mitigation capacity.

subtasks.title: Consider DDoS mitigation service
subtasks.content: Considered engaging a specialized DDoS mitigation service (e.g., Cloudflare, Akamai, AWS Shield) for large-scale attacks.

subtasks.title: Consider network security experts
subtasks.content: Considered retaining network security experts to assist with traffic analysis and mitigation strategy.

subtasks.title: Consider ORNA support
subtasks.content: Considered retaining ORNA for incident response expertise.

tasks.title: Assess Impact
tasks.content: Assess the operational, financial, and reputational impact of the service disruption to prioritize response actions.

subtasks.title: Assess business disruption
subtasks.content: Assessed impact to business operations: Which services are unavailable? How many customers are affected? What revenue is at risk?

subtasks.title: Assess service degradation
subtasks.content: Assessed the degree of service degradation and whether partial functionality can be maintained during the attack.

subtasks.title: Assess financial impact
subtasks.content: Assessed financial impact including lost revenue, SLA penalties, and costs of mitigation services.

subtasks.title: Assess reputational damage
subtasks.content: Assessed potential reputational damage from service outages and customer impact.

tasks.title: Determine Root Cause and Attack Motivation
tasks.content: Investigate how the attack is being executed and potential motivations to inform mitigation strategy.

subtasks.title: Analyze attack patterns
subtasks.content: Analyzed attack patterns to determine if this is an opportunistic attack, targeted attack, extortion attempt, or smokescreen for other malicious activity.

subtasks.title: Check for extortion demands
subtasks.content: Checked for any extortion demands or ransom notes that may accompany the DDoS attack.

subtasks.title: Review threat intelligence
subtasks.content: Reviewed threat intelligence feeds to see if similar attacks are targeting other organizations in the same sector.

subtasks.title: Investigate botnet involvement
subtasks.content: Investigated whether the attack is using a known botnet (e.g., Mirai, Memcached amplification).

tasks.title: Send Communications
tasks.content: Timely communications to internal stakeholders and potentially customers about service disruptions are critical for managing expectations.
outcomes: Internal stakeholders are notified of the incident
Status page is updated if applicable

subtasks.title: Work with Legal Counsel
subtasks.content: Worked with Legal Counsel and provided all available details about the incident severity and impact.

subtasks.title: Notify internal stakeholders
subtasks.content: Notified internal stakeholders including executives, customer support teams, and operations teams.

subtasks.title: Update status page
subtasks.content: Updated public status page or service dashboard to inform customers of ongoing service issues.

subtasks.title: Prepare customer communications
subtasks.content: Prepared customer communications explaining service disruptions without revealing technical details that could aid attackers.

subtasks.title: Utilize ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Utilized ORNA communications templates where appropriate.

phases.name: Containment
phases.description: The main objective of the Containment stage is to limit the impact of the attack and begin restoring service availability.

tasks.title: Implement Immediate Traffic Filtering
tasks.content: Deploy immediate filtering rules to block or rate-limit attack traffic at network perimeter devices.
outcomes: Attack traffic is being filtered at the perimeter

subtasks.title: Configure firewall rules
subtasks.content: Configured firewall rules to block identified attack source IP addresses, geographic regions, or suspicious traffic patterns.

subtasks.title: Implement rate limiting
subtasks.content: Implemented rate limiting on affected services to prevent resource exhaustion.

subtasks.title: Enable connection limits
subtasks.content: Enabled connection limits and timeout settings to prevent connection table exhaustion.

subtasks.title: Deploy IPS signatures
subtasks.content: Deployed IPS/IDS signatures to detect and block specific attack patterns.

tasks.title: Activate DDoS Mitigation Services
tasks.content: If available, activate cloud-based DDoS mitigation or scrubbing services to filter attack traffic upstream.
outcomes: Traffic is being scrubbed before reaching infrastructure

subtasks.title: Activate cloud DDoS protection
subtasks.content: Activated cloud-based DDoS protection service (e.g., Cloudflare, AWS Shield, Azure DDoS Protection).

subtasks.title: Redirect DNS to scrubbing service
subtasks.content: Redirected DNS records to route traffic through scrubbing service if using DNS-based mitigation.

subtasks.title: Configure BGP routing
subtasks.content: Configured BGP routing changes to redirect traffic through mitigation provider if using network-layer mitigation.

subtasks.title: Coordinate with ISP
subtasks.content: Coordinated with ISP to implement upstream filtering or traffic diversion.

tasks.title: Scale Infrastructure Resources
tasks.content: Increase infrastructure capacity to absorb attack traffic and maintain service availability where possible.

subtasks.title: Scale compute resources
subtasks.content: Scaled up compute resources (servers, instances) if using cloud infrastructure with auto-scaling capabilities.

subtasks.title: Increase bandwidth capacity
subtasks.content: Increased bandwidth capacity or activated additional transit providers if available.

subtasks.title: Deploy additional load balancers
subtasks.content: Deployed additional load balancers or CDN edge nodes to distribute attack impact.

subtasks.title: Activate failover systems
subtasks.content: Activated failover or backup systems to maintain critical service availability.

tasks.title: Implement Application-Level Protections
tasks.content: Deploy application-level defenses for Layer 7 (application layer) attacks targeting web services.

subtasks.title: Enable WAF rules
subtasks.content: Enabled Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to block malicious HTTP requests and bot traffic.

subtasks.title: Implement CAPTCHA challenges
subtasks.content: Implemented CAPTCHA or JavaScript challenges to distinguish human users from bots.

subtasks.title: Configure HTTP rate limiting
subtasks.content: Configured HTTP rate limiting and request throttling at the application or reverse proxy level.

subtasks.title: Deploy bot management
subtasks.content: Deployed bot management solutions to identify and block automated attack traffic.

tasks.title: Invoke Business Continuity Plan
tasks.content: Activate business continuity procedures to maintain critical operations during the attack.

subtasks.title: Activate BCP
subtasks.content: Activated Business Continuity Plan procedures for service disruption scenarios.

subtasks.title: Prioritize critical services
subtasks.content: Prioritized critical services and allocated resources to maintain essential business functions.

subtasks.title: Coordinate response teams
subtasks.content: Coordinated response teams including network operations, security, and customer support.

subtasks.title: Document mitigation actions
subtasks.content: Documented all mitigation actions taken for incident reporting and post-incident review.

tasks.title: Issue Communications Updates
tasks.content: Provide regular updates to stakeholders on mitigation progress and service status.

subtasks.title: Update internal stakeholders
subtasks.content: Provided updates to internal stakeholders on containment progress and expected timelines.

subtasks.title: Update status page
subtasks.content: Updated public status page with current service status and mitigation progress.

subtasks.title: Communicate with customers
subtasks.content: Communicated with affected customers about service restoration efforts.

phases.name: Eradication
phases.description: The Eradication stage focuses on fully stopping the attack and ensuring attackers cannot resume the assault.

tasks.title: Analyze and Block Attack Infrastructure
tasks.content: Identify and block the infrastructure being used to conduct the attack, including botnets and command-and-control servers.
outcomes: Attack infrastructure is identified and blocked

subtasks.title: Identify botnet C&C servers
subtasks.content: Identified command-and-control servers coordinating the botnet attack through traffic analysis.

subtasks.title: Block C&C communications
subtasks.content: Blocked communications to identified C&C servers at the firewall and DNS level.

subtasks.title: Report to authorities
subtasks.content: Reported botnet infrastructure to appropriate authorities and abuse contacts for takedown efforts.

subtasks.title: Share threat intelligence
subtasks.content: Shared indicators of compromise with security community and threat intelligence platforms.

tasks.title: Harden Network and Service Configurations
tasks.content: Implement long-term hardening measures to make systems more resilient to future attacks.
outcomes: Services are hardened against future attacks

subtasks.title: Implement SYN cookies
subtasks.content: Implemented SYN cookies and other TCP stack hardening to resist SYN flood attacks.

subtasks.title: Configure connection limits
subtasks.content: Configured appropriate connection limits and timeouts on all internet-facing services.

subtasks.title: Disable unnecessary services
subtasks.content: Disabled unnecessary services and protocols that could be exploited in amplification attacks.

subtasks.title: Implement BCP38 filtering
subtasks.content: Implemented BCP38 (ingress filtering) to prevent IP spoofing where possible.

tasks.title: Optimize Architecture for Resilience
tasks.content: Review and optimize architecture to improve resilience against future DDoS attacks.

subtasks.title: Implement geographic distribution
subtasks.content: Implemented geographic distribution of services using CDN or multi-region deployment.

subtasks.title: Deploy anycast routing
subtasks.content: Deployed anycast routing to distribute attack traffic across multiple locations.

subtasks.title: Implement caching
subtasks.content: Implemented aggressive caching strategies to reduce load on origin servers.

subtasks.title: Separate infrastructure
subtasks.content: Separated critical infrastructure from public-facing services where possible.

tasks.title: Fine-Tune Detection and Alerting
tasks.content: Improve monitoring and alerting to detect future attacks more quickly.

subtasks.title: Configure baseline monitoring
subtasks.content: Configured baseline monitoring for normal traffic patterns to quickly identify anomalies.

subtasks.title: Set up alerting thresholds
subtasks.content: Set up appropriate alerting thresholds for traffic volume, connection rates, and service response times.

subtasks.title: Deploy flow analysis
subtasks.content: Deployed NetFlow or sFlow analysis tools for detailed traffic visibility.

subtasks.title: Integrate threat intelligence
subtasks.content: Integrated threat intelligence feeds to proactively block known attack sources.

phases.name: Recovery
phases.description: The Recovery stage aims to fully restore normal service operation and verify system stability.

tasks.title: Restore Full Service Availability
tasks.content: Gradually restore services to full capacity while monitoring for any signs of attack resumption.

subtasks.title: Verify attack has ceased
subtasks.content: Verified that attack traffic has stopped and normal traffic patterns have resumed.

subtasks.title: Remove temporary restrictions
subtasks.content: Gradually removed temporary traffic restrictions and rate limits while monitoring for issues.

subtasks.title: Scale down resources
subtasks.content: Scaled down temporarily increased infrastructure resources to normal levels.

subtasks.title: Test service functionality
subtasks.content: Tested all service functionality to ensure complete restoration of capabilities.

tasks.title: Validate System Performance
tasks.content: Verify that all systems are performing normally and no degradation remains.

subtasks.title: Monitor performance metrics
subtasks.content: Monitored key performance metrics including latency, throughput, and error rates.

subtasks.title: Verify customer access
subtasks.content: Verified that customers can access all services without issues.

subtasks.title: Check for collateral damage
subtasks.content: Checked for any collateral damage to systems or data from the attack or mitigation efforts.

subtasks.title: Validate security controls
subtasks.content: Validated that all security controls are functioning properly post-recovery.

tasks.title: Maintain Enhanced Monitoring
tasks.content: Continue enhanced monitoring for a period after attack cessation to detect any resumption.
outcomes: Attack resumption would be quickly detected

subtasks.title: Maintain heightened alerting
subtasks.content: Maintained heightened alerting thresholds for a period after the attack.

subtasks.title: Monitor for follow-up attacks
subtasks.content: Monitored for potential follow-up attacks or attack pattern changes.

subtasks.title: Review daily traffic reports
subtasks.content: Reviewed daily traffic analysis reports for anomalies.

tasks.title: Issue Final Communications
tasks.content: Communicate service restoration and thank stakeholders for their patience.
outcomes: Stakeholders are informed of full restoration

subtasks.title: Update status page
subtasks.content: Updated status page to reflect full service restoration.

subtasks.title: Notify internal stakeholders
subtasks.content: Notified internal stakeholders that incident response is concluding.

subtasks.title: Communicate with customers
subtasks.content: Communicated with customers about service restoration and any follow-up actions.

tasks.title: Resolve Incident
tasks.content: Formally close the incident once all services are restored and stable.
outcomes: The incident is declared as resolved

subtasks.title: Verify complete restoration
subtasks.content: Verified with all stakeholders that services are fully restored and performing normally.

subtasks.title: Change incident status
subtasks.content: Changed status of incident to 'resolved'.

phases.name: Lessons Learned
phases.description: The Lessons Learned stage's main goal is to compile all relevant information about the incident and extract lessons for improving future response.

tasks.title: Generate Incident Report
tasks.content: Create a comprehensive incident report documenting the attack, response actions, and outcomes.

subtasks.title: Generate report
subtasks.content: Generated the incident report using the Reports tab.

subtasks.title: Review for accuracy
subtasks.content: Reviewed the report for accuracy and completeness.

subtasks.title: Include attack metrics
subtasks.content: Included detailed attack metrics (volume, duration, vectors, sources) in the report.

subtasks.title: Document mitigation actions
subtasks.content: Documented all mitigation actions taken and their effectiveness.

subtasks.title: Share report
subtasks.content: Shared the report with appropriate stakeholders.

tasks.title: Conduct Cost Analysis
tasks.content: Analyze the financial impact of the attack and response efforts.
outcomes: Total cost of incident is calculated
ROI of mitigation investments is evaluated

subtasks.title: Calculate direct costs
subtasks.content: Calculated direct costs including mitigation service fees, additional bandwidth, and overtime.

subtasks.title: Calculate revenue impact
subtasks.content: Calculated revenue impact from service downtime and customer attrition.

subtasks.title: Assess insurance coverage
subtasks.content: Assessed insurance coverage and filed claims if applicable.

subtasks.title: Evaluate mitigation ROI
subtasks.content: Evaluated return on investment of DDoS mitigation measures.

tasks.title: Host Lessons Learned Session
tasks.content: Conduct a lessons learned session to identify improvements to DDoS preparedness and response.

subtasks.title: Prepare presentation
subtasks.content: Prepared presentation covering attack timeline, response effectiveness, and improvement opportunities.

subtasks.title: Host session
subtasks.content: Hosted the lessons learned session with all key stakeholders.

subtasks.title: Discuss response effectiveness
subtasks.content: Discussed what worked well and what could be improved in the response process.

subtasks.title: Identify infrastructure gaps
subtasks.content: Identified gaps in infrastructure resilience and mitigation capabilities.

subtasks.title: Create improvement plan
subtasks.content: Created an action plan for implementing identified improvements.

tasks.title: Update DDoS Response Plan
tasks.content: Update the DDoS response plan based on lessons learned from this incident.

subtasks.title: Update response procedures
subtasks.content: Updated response procedures based on lessons learned.

subtasks.title: Update contact lists
subtasks.content: Updated contact lists for ISPs, mitigation vendors, and internal stakeholders.

subtasks.title: Update runbooks
subtasks.content: Updated technical runbooks with improved mitigation steps.

subtasks.title: Schedule training
subtasks.content: Scheduled training exercises to practice updated response procedures.

name: Insider Threat Response
description: Comprehensive playbook for responding to insider threat incidents involving employees, contractors, or business associates who misuse their authorized access to harm the organization.

phases.name: Identification
phases.description: The purpose of the Identification stage is to accurately determine the Incident type, severity and perform immediate containment and escalation.

tasks.title: Initiate CIRT Team Meeting and Execute Escalation Procedures
tasks.content: As more information about the incident is known, the core CIRT team will meet and define which organizational SMEs need to be included to respond to the incident. Insider threat incidents require special handling due to legal, privacy, and personnel considerations.
outcomes: First Core CIRT meeting is held with invited SMEs
OOB communications channels are set up and periodic checkins scheduled
Privileged Communications is established with legal counsel
HR and Legal are involved from the beginning

subtasks.title: Initiate first CIRT meeting
subtasks.content: Initiated first meeting of the organizational core CIRT.

subtasks.title: Notify CIRT team members
subtasks.content: Notified CIRT team members using the contacts and role assignments on the My Team page and made sure ORNA roles are assigned so they are aware of the tasks allocated for them.

subtasks.title: Immediately involve HR and Legal
subtasks.content: Immediately involved HR and Legal Counsel given the sensitive nature of insider threat investigations and potential employment law implications.

subtasks.title: Verify Incident Severity
subtasks.content: Verified the Incident Severity determined during the Incident record creation process as promptly and accurately as possible. The severity may change as more details are uncovered and it will need to be updated when the situation changes.

subtasks.title: Invite additional SMEs
subtasks.content: Invited additional SMEs to be included in CIRT update meetings.

subtasks.title: Set up secured meeting room
subtasks.content: Set up a secured meeting room (either physical and/or virtual) with strict confidentiality.

subtasks.title: Establish strict need-to-know policy
subtasks.content: Established strict need-to-know policy for all information about the investigation to protect employee privacy and investigation integrity.

subtasks.title: Work with Legal Counsel
subtasks.content: Worked closely with Legal Counsel to retain client-attorney privilege over the information pertaining to the incident and ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations including employment law.

subtasks.title: Create initial incident assessment
subtasks.content: Created an initial incident assessment to share with organizational leaders. This assessment notification will contain a summary of what has occurred; date & time of incident; scope of compromise; data involved; actions taken so far and next steps recipients can expect to see.

tasks.title: Validate Insider Threat Incident
tasks.content: An insider threat is a security risk that originates from within the organization. It involves employees, former employees, contractors, or business associates who have inside information concerning the organization's security practices, data and computer systems.
outcomes: A security incident is confirmed as an insider threat
Type of insider threat is determined (malicious, negligent, compromised)

subtasks.title: Review initial indicators
subtasks.content: Reviewed initial indicators that triggered the investigation including: unusual access patterns, data exfiltration alerts, policy violations, or behavioral concerns reported by managers or colleagues.

subtasks.title: Analyze user activity logs
subtasks.content: Analyzed user activity logs, file access logs, email logs, and system logs for suspicious patterns or unauthorized activity.

subtasks.title: Determine threat type
subtasks.content: Determined whether this is: malicious insider (intentional harm), negligent insider (careless behavior), or compromised insider (account taken over by external actor).

subtasks.title: Gather preliminary evidence
subtasks.content: Gathered preliminary evidence while maintaining chain of custody and following legal requirements for employee monitoring and investigation.

tasks.title: Determine Scope of Insider Activity
